title: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei calls OpenAI’s messaging around military deal ‘straight up lies,’ report says
summary: Anthropic gave up its contract with the Pentagon over AI safety disagreements -- then, OpenAI swooped in.

title: Apple Music to add Transparency Tags to distinguish AI music, says report
summary: The label or distributor has to opt in to tagging their music as AI, so it's unclear how effective this intervention will be.

title: Google settles with Epic Games, drops its Play Store commissions to 20%
summary: Google has dropped its commission, charging a 20% service fee and an optional 5% to use its billing services. It will also offer a new process for third-party app stores.

title: His house burned down. He used the insurance money to build PopSockets.
summary: Does a consumer hardware company need to get on the VC treadmill to succeed? Eleven years and 290 million products sold across 115 countries later, PopSockets has proven that the bootstrapped, low-dilution path more viable than the industry gives it credit for. The global consumer hardware brand was built on less than $500k, no institutional capital, and a philosophy professor’s determination.  Watch as founder and former CEO […]

title: US and EU police shut down LeakBase, a site accused of sharing stolen passwords and hacking tools
summary: Authorities say LeakBase was "one of the world’s largest online forums for cybercriminals," and maintained an archive of hacked databases containing hundreds of millions of passwords.

title: MacBook Neo, iPhone 17e, and everything else Apple announced this week
summary: From a new iPhone to a budget-friendly MacBook, Apple announced a series of new products this week.

title: Google Search rolls out Gemini’s Canvas in AI Mode to all US users
summary: Canvas in AI Mode is available to U.S. users in English for creating plans, projects, apps, and more.

title: Decagon completes first tender offer at $4.5B valuation
summary: The AI-powered customer support startup is the latest example of a fast-growing, young company that's providing employee liquidity.

title: The US military is still using Claude — but defense-tech clients are fleeing
summary: As the U.S. continues its aerial attack on Iran, Anthropic models are being used for many targeting decisions.

title: X taps William Shatner to give out invites to its payments service, X Money
summary: 42 X users donated to William Shatner's charity in exchange for an early invite to X Money beta.

title: Host a Side Event during TechCrunch Founder Summit Week in Boston
summary: Want to tap into the energy of 1,100+ startup founders, investors, and tech leaders descending on Boston for the Founder Summit 2026 on June 9? Host your own Side Event during “Founder Summit Week,” happening June 4-10!

title: TikTok won’t add end-to-end encryption to direct messages, report says
summary: The social media giant says that end-to-end encryption would make users less safe.

title: Father sues Google, claiming Gemini chatbot drove son into fatal delusion
summary: A father is suing Google and Alphabet, alleging its Gemini chatbot reinforced his son’s delusional belief it was his AI wife and coached him toward suicide and a planned airport attack.

title: Meet the MacBook Neo, Apple’s colorful answer to the Chromebook, starting at $599
summary: Apple is offering the MacBook Neo at a more affordable price point by using an A18 Pro chip, which is used in the iPhone 16 Pro.

title: One startup’s pitch to provide more reliable AI answers: Crowdsource the chatbots
summary: CollectivIQ looks to give users more accurate answers to their AI queries by showing them responses that pull information from ChatGPT, Gemini, Claude, Grok — and up to 10 other models — all at the same time.

title: Who needs data centers in space when they can float offshore?
summary: Offshore wind developer Aikido will deploy a small data center beneath a floating offshore wind turbine later this year.

title: Eight Sleep raises $50M at $1.5B valuation
summary: Eight Sleep said it was free-cash-flow positive in 2025 and plans to use the new funding for new products, global expansions, and clinical validation.

title: Why AI startups are selling the same equity at two different prices
summary: Some AI founders are using a novel valuation mechanism to manufacture unicorn status.

title: A suite of government hacking tools targeting iPhones is now being used by cybercriminals
summary: Security researchers say exploits used by governments to hack into iPhones have been found to be used by cybercriminals. They warned of an emerging market for "secondhand" exploits.

title: Alibaba’s Qwen tech lead steps down after major AI push
summary: Reactions rippled through Alibaba's Qwen team after tech lead Junyang Lin stepped down following a major model launch.

title: Jensen Huang says Nvidia is pulling back from OpenAI and Anthropic, but his explanation raises more questions than it answers
summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang said Wednesday that his company's investments in OpenAI and Anthropic will likely be its last — but his explanation may not tell the whole story.
